name: wilson-tree
description: Junk-drawer guard for AI agents — a project that has fired N experiments tends to scatter them across a flat `state/<§N_…>/` tree plus an ever-growing inline progress log; an agent that re-enters the repo has to re-grep the whole world. wilson-tree teaches three habits at the hook layer. (P1 inject) SessionStart / PostCompact / UserPromptSubmit reads the project's `REGISTRY.md` (one line per §N: id · topic · status · path · verdict-slug) and the nearest `AGENTS.md` and injects a compact summary so the agent walks in with the map instead of having to discover it. (P2 register) PostToolUse(Write) on `state/**/result.json` auto-classifies the new §N by filename keyword into one of the configured topics and appends a `REGISTRY.md` row (heuristic: directory name contains a topic slug; ambiguous → UNCLASSIFIED, surfaced for the slash command to fix). (P3 guard) PreToolUse(Write|Edit|MultiEdit) on `state/**` outside `<tree_root>/<topic>/state/` emits a one-line warning by default; `SIDECAR_TREE_GUARD_STRICT=1` upgrades it to a deny. Inert until `REGISTRY.md` exists OR `.wilson-tree.json` is present. Slash: /wilson-tree status|audit|topics|register|off. Opt out: SIDECAR_NO_TREE=1.
